'''Waisale Sovatabua''' (born 26 June 1973) is a Fijian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|fb|wg}} or {{rlp|ce}} in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at representative level for [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]], and at club level for the [[Sheffield Eagles (1984)|Sheffield Eagles]], [[Huddersfield Giants]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]]. He hails from the highland province of Namosi [village of Navunikabi] on Viti Levu with maternal links to the village of Ketei, Totoya, Lau archipelago.

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Waisale Sovatabua played {{rlp|fb}} in Sheffield Eagles' 17-8 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1998 Challenge Cup]] Final during [[Super League III]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 2 May 1998.

In 2003, he switched to [[rugby union]], playing for [[Otley R.U.F.C.|Otley]],<ref>{{cite web |title=Otley warm up in style with 42 points |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/8010460.otley-warmup-in-stylewith-42points/ |website=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |access-date=17 August 2023 |date=28 August 2003}}</ref> before returning to rugby league and finishing career with a second spell at Sheffield Eagles.

===International career===
He was a [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fijian]] international and played at the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup|1995]] and [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]]s.<ref name="The Teams: Fiji">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport/hi/english/static/in_depth/rugby_league/2000/world_cup/team_profiles/fiji.stm|title=The Teams: Fiji|publisher=BBC|date=2000-01-01|access-date=2008-05-05|archive-date=20 December 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071220033421/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport/hi/english/static/in_depth/rugby_league/2000/world_cup/team_profiles/fiji.stm|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="World Cup 1995 details">{{cite web|url=http://www2.hunterlink.net.au/~maajjs/res/wcup1995.htm|title=World Cup 1995 details|publisher=Hunterlinks|date=1995-01-01|access-date=2008-05-05|archive-date=29 September 2007|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070929104732/http://www2.hunterlink.net.au/~maajjs/res/wcup1995.htm|url-status=dead}}</ref>
